Tests if `Boolean.prototype` is the prototype of a new `Boolean` object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion that checks if the `Boolean` prototype is part of the prototype chain of a newly created `Boolean` object. The `assert` function is used to verify that `Boolean.prototype.isPrototypeOf(new Boolean(true))` returns `true`, confirming that the `new Boolean(true)` object is indeed an instance of the `Boolean` prototype. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on validating the relationship between a `Boolean` object and its prototype. It is likely part of a test suite or a validation script to ensure correct behavior of JavaScript's prototype inheritance for `Boolean` objects.
